content.full_text:
84305

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95809 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

March 16, 1976

John L. Kennedy
Chairman
Carpenter Campaign Committee
5851 Karen Avenue
Cypress, California 90630

A- 76-362

Dear Mr. Kennedy:

The Fair Political Practices Commission acknowledges
receipt on March 8, 1976, of your letter concerning a
campaign piece for Democratic candidate Deketa Durham in
the 37th Senate District.

Your informal complaint alleges a violation of Sec-
tion 12047 of the Elections Code. Since the Commission is
not given jurisdiction to administer the Elections Code,
we will be unable to accept your complaint. I am forward-
ing your letter and attachment to John Gordnier, Deputy
Attorney General, 555 Capitol Mall, Sacramento, California.

The Political Reform Act of 1974 does contain provi-
sions regarding mass mailings. Under Section 84305 of the
Political Reform Act of 1974, any person required to file
campaign reports who sends a mass mailing ("[200] or more
identical or nearly identical pieces of mail", Section
82401.5)" in support of or in opposition to a state candi-
date or state ballot measure" must send a copy of the mass
mailing to the Commission on the day the mailing is sent
to the public. All mass mailings must include the sender's
name, street address and city listed on one of the inserts
(Section 84305 as amended by AB 1259) or be sent by bulk
rate or metered mail. While the facts stated in your let-
ter are insufficient to determine whether the submitted
mailing was a "mass mailing" and if Citizens for Deketa
Durham had filing obligations under the Political Reform
Act so as to require submission of a copy of the mass mail-
ing to the Commission, the identification on the mailer

The image provided appears to be almost entirely blank or white, with only very faint markings or shadows visible at the top. There is no clearly legible text content to transcribe from this document.

John L. Kennedy
Page Two                                    March 16, 1976


apparently complied with the Political Reform Act of 1974.

     Feel free to contact me if you have any questions.

                                             Sincerely,

                                             [illegible signature]
                                             Barbara Campbell
                                             Enforcement Division

BC:is

cc:  John Gordnier

I can see this is a mostly blank page with just two circular holes or marks at the top. There is no readable text content to transcribe from this image.

CARPENTER
CAMPAIGN
COMMITTEE

5851 Karen Avenue, Cypress, CA 90630

John L. Kennedy, Chairman

ID # 742391                                    (714) 995 - 2342


February 29, 1976


Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95814

Ladies and Gentlemen:

     I am enclosing a copy of a campaign piece for Deketa
Durham, candidate for the Democratic nomination for the 37th
State Senate District. I believe this piece to be in violation of
Section 12047 of the Elections Code, which requires any campaign
material "which is designed to injure or defeat any candidate for
nomination or election to any public office by reflecting upon his
personal character or political action to have, in a conspicuous
place, the name and address of the printer and the name and
address of the chairman and secretary or the names and addresses
of at least two officers of the political or other organization
issuing it."

     This piece appears to be designed to defeat Paul
Carpenter, also a candidate for the Democratic nomination for
the 37th State Senate seat. The piece does not have the required
two names and addresses of officers of the organization issuing it.

     Please let me know if there is a form which I must
file to make this a formal complaint of a violation of the Elections
Code.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        JOHN L. KENNEDY
                                        Chairman

JLK/j

Enc.


PRECISION LITHO EAST LTD. W. Washington Blvd • Los Angeles ⟵→ 103

The image appears to be almost entirely blank or extremely faded, with only two small curved marks visible near the top of the page. The rest of the page is white/blank space with no legible text content to transcribe.

Citizens for

Deketa Durham

7980 Geranium Circle
Buena Park, California 90620

(714) 870-9811

Candidate

37th Senate District

Dear Fellow Citizens:

We need a change!

A change from politics as usual . . . a change from self-seeking politicians who represent themselves first and you last . . . and a change from the big-money bulldozing of special-interest candidates into office.

As Democrats, we face the public spectacle of grand jury investigations into the political and business affairs of Orange County Democratic Chairman Richard O'Neill and his partner, mystery money-man Dr. Louis Cella. Though their outrageously heavy contributions, these two individuals have effectively elected such current officials as Assemblyman Paul Carpenter and Richard Robinson, and Supervisor Robert Battin (himself under indictment and trial at the present time!)

The hard fact is that if we're to turn from politics-as-usual, we must first turn from politicians-as-usual! To have officeholders who can't be bought when they're in office, we must elect candidates who aren't bought during the campaign.

We need new blood.

First, we need representatives who will stay in close touch with you after the election. . . not just seek your vote before it. Second, we need representatives who will serve the interests of the people in the tradition of the Democratic Party, instead of feathering their own nests and spending their tax-paid time seeking higher offices. To do this, we need candidates of the people, who are supported by the people, and who are responsible to nobody but the people. The people themselves must do the electing, not the big-money men and the back-room bosses.

We are fortunate to have such a candidate in Deketa Durham, who seeks the Democratic nomination in the 57th State Senate District. Deketa is a wife and mother, a successful businesswoman, and carries with her the attitude of the voter and grass-roots citizen.

Deketa believes that the business of government is protecting us from threats to our person and our families — violent crime, narcotics, pornography, and government snooping — not protecting us from ourselves by making all our daily decisions for us. Like Governor Brown, Deketa believes that our personal decisions should be outside the realm of government, and that the government should serve us, not rule us.

She is a fresh face! Beholden to no one, owned by no one, she will represent you and do it well. Because Deketa's campaign opposes the big-boss, big-money approach, she will receive no backing from those traditional corners. For her to win, for you to win through her election, we need you as a part of this campaign.

We neither want nor need big contributors, because they're never free. We need instead the support of the forgotten voters who have finally decided to do something besides talk. Your modest help, with that of many others to whom this message is sent, will help restore honesty in government.

Sign up now as a member of this campaign. All you need to do is send a dollar a week (we'll pay postage both ways and keep you informed about the campaign), or make a one-time contribution of five dollars or more. Call us for a pre-stamped envelope, and speed Deketa's people-to-people campaign on its way!

Yours for better government,

Nuel W. Brown
Campaign Chairman
Citizens for Deketa Durham

P.S. We also seek the help of all willing volunteer workers. Call us at 870-9811, and we'll be glad to put you to work!

PRINTED BY — DURHAM'S CORNER RUSH PRINT & ADVERTISING 1351-K So. Beach Blvd., La Habra, CA 90631

[The image appears to be a blank yellow/cream colored page with two white circular fasteners at the top and one small brown dot/fastener in the middle-lower area. There is no visible text to transcribe.]
